source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting, MP 311 B

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Main Pass Block 311

event.top_event: Rupture of pressurized gas lift injection tubing with personnel injury
actions_taken: Production casing valves closed and pressure isolated
First aid provided to injured personnel
Pump Through Plugs (PTPs) inspected and replaced
New pressure gauges and needle valves installed
Operators required to manually verify casing pressures
event.summary: On 12 March 2023 at 1633 hours, two Louisiana Safety Systems technicians were injured on MP 311 B platform while performing valve maintenance on well QAY B-18ST. A 1-inch stainless steel gas lift injection tubing, rated at approximately 3100 psi, ruptured due to 4330 psi of unexpected pressure trapped in the tubing. One technician suffered a serious injury resulting in lost time greater than 3 days; the other sustained minor injuries. The incident was caused by: (1) failure to monitor casing pressure, (2) failure to perform pre-job hazard walkthrough, (3) failure to perform required casing diagnostic, and (4) discrepancies between approved drawings and actual field installation. Root cause was downhole well integrity issues that resulted in casing pressure buildup following a grease zerk repair in July 2021.
recommendations: Generate Safety Alert emphasizing importance of accurate critical pressure data readings
Ensure operators of dry trees on gas lift verify that tubing and valves are rated per approved drawings
Implement mandatory pre-job hazard walkthroughs before commencing work
Establish routine casing pressure monitoring at required intervals
Perform casing diagnostics when trigger pressures are reached
Verify facility piping and tubing installation matches approved drawings
Implement physical pressure gauge verification instead of relying solely on SCADA system readings

threats.name: Casing pressure buildup due to downhole integrity failure
Overpressure of tubing rated below actual system pressure
Technicians unaware of hazardous pressure conditions
Tubing rupture from pressure exceeding design rating

consequences.name: Personnel struck by high-velocity escaping gas
Serious injury to technician (lost time > 3 days)
Casing flange leak and environmental concern

controls.name: Pre-job Safety Analysis (JSA) and hazard walkthrough
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and communicate risks to workers before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent workers from entering hazardous situation unaware

supporting_text: The failure to perform a pre-job hazard walkthrough was confirmed in the GOM Shelf's internal investigative report
they were told by the PIC, 'go figure it out, do your job'
The LSS Technicians were unaware of the hazard (extremely high gas pressure, 4330 psi) upstream of the 2 inch production casing valve prior to starting their job

controls.name: Casing pressure monitoring and trending
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal casing pressures before they reach dangerous levels
controls.lod_basis: Continuous monitoring to detect pressure buildup and trigger corrective action

supporting_text: GOM Shelf failed to monitor the casing pressure. This led to the workers not knowing the pressure that was on the casing
Operators were found to have not been monitoring casing pressures at the required intervals

A 3-day trending chart, obtained prior to the incident, printed out zero (0) pressures on the B-18 production casing. Because the casing valves were closed, this 0 psi reading on the SCADA system was inaccurate, and not indicative of the actual casing pressures on the well

controls.name: Casing diagnostic when trigger pressure reached
controls.barrier_role: Identify downhole well integrity issues and prevent pressure buildup
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement to diagnose well condition when pressure thresholds exceeded

supporting_text: GOM Shelf failed to carry out a vital casing pressure diagnostic, resulting in the incident. Had GOM Shelf performed this diagnostic, any downhole issues in the well could have been identified and resolved, eliminating the root cause of the casing pressure
Human Performance Error: Not following procedures. Operators failed to perform a casing diagnostic as required by regulations
A casing diagnostic was not performed when the trigger pressure was reached post grease zerk remediation

controls.name: Verification of facility design against approved drawings
controls.barrier_role: Ensure installed equipment matches design specifications and pressure ratings
controls.lod_basis: Design verification to prevent installation of undersized equipment

supporting_text: The platform piping and tubing was not built to the approved drawings. This directly led to the overpressure event when the casing pressure was introduced to the 1-inch tubing
BSEE reviewed the currently approved drawings for the facility and found multiple inconsistencies with the installation and the drawings
The gas lift tubing was actually rated for 3100 psi
the approved SFDs for the facility only show one casing valve, but the well actually had 2 casing valves

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) system activation
controls.barrier_role: Rapidly isolate pressure and stop gas flow following rupture
controls.lod_basis: Automated and manual shutdown to limit consequences after rupture occurs

supporting_text: IP #2 headed up the stairs to get help and activated one of the Emergency Shutdown (ESD) stations along the way
Personnel on the top deck, after hearing the loud explosion, immediately activated the ESD as well
A GOM Shelf Operator then closed the production casing valves on the B-18 well and isolated the pressure so that the remaining gas in the line bled down

controls.name: Manual pressure verification by operators
controls.barrier_role: Verify actual casing pressures through direct measurement rather than relying on automated systems
controls.lod_basis: Direct physical verification to detect SCADA system failures or sensing line isolation

supporting_text: the GOM Shelf operators had not been verifying casing pressures. Instead, they were relying on their SCADA system report which depended on a sensing line that was isolated from the casing pressure
New pressure gauges and needle valves have been installed on all well casings and the operators are required to physically open and close the valves when pressure readings are being taken
Operators are no longer allowed to only observe the casing pressure readings in the control room off the SCADA System

controls.name: Tubing and valve pressure rating verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all components are rated for maximum expected system pressure
controls.lod_basis: Design control to prevent installation of undersized equipment

supporting_text: the 1 inch SS production casing tubing line (rated for approximately 3100 psi) was overpressured by the 4330 psi flowing from the well into the production casing
Recommend operators of dry trees on gas lift ensure that their tubing and valves are rated per their approved drawings

controls.name: First aid and emergency medical response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care to minimize injury severity
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response to stabilize injuries and arrange evacuation

supporting_text: IP #1 was placed on a bed and provided first aid to minimize his pain as much as possible
The Medic provided medical attention to IP #1 to stabilize his injuries and then administered an IV
The Air-Med arrived at MP 311 B. The Medical Technician arrived and prepared IP #1 for travel and then the Air-Med helicopter departed at 2135 hours for University Medical Center (UMC) in New Orleans

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
